name: Lemon Lavender
desc: Meyer lemon zest meets culinary lavender in a brown-butter base. Made with peak-summer lavender from an Oregon farm and year-round Meyer lemons. 47 test batches. Priya's personal favorite.

name: Pumpkin Spice Chai
desc: Real pumpkin purée, house-blended chai spices, and a maple glaze. Sold out in 4 days. We underestimated pumpkin people.

name: Peppermint Bark
desc: Dark chocolate base with crushed candy cane and a white chocolate drizzle. Holiday-only. Came with a tiny candy cane taped to the box.

name: Cherry Blossom
desc: Sakura-infused dough with dried cherries and a matcha drizzle. Made in collaboration with Yuki's Tokyo patisserie. Our first international collab.

name: Maple Pecan Crunch
desc: Vermont maple syrup, candied pecans, and a brown butter base with flaky salt. Marcus's recipe. Won our internal blind taste test 11-1.

name: Gingerbread Stout
desc: Molasses, fresh ginger, and a splash of local stout beer in the dough. Finished with a stout glaze. Developed with a Portland brewery.

name: Strawberry Basil
desc: Freeze-dried strawberries and fresh basil in a vanilla bean dough. Our very first seasonal flavor. Sold out in 48 hours.

name: Hot Honey Cornbread
desc: Cornmeal cookie with hot honey drizzle and a hint of lime zest. Sweet, spicy, and utterly unexpected. Diego's brainchild.

name: Marionberry Crumble
desc: Oregon's own marionberries in a brown butter crumble cookie. Tart, sweet, and purple-stained. Made with berries picked 30 miles from the kitchen.

name: Apple Cider Donut
desc: Reduced apple cider from Hood River orchards, cinnamon sugar coating, and a cake-donut texture. Tastes like a Vermont orchard in October.

name: Eggnog Rum
desc: Fresh-grated nutmeg, a splash of dark rum in the dough, and a rum glaze. Our holiday party cookie. Strictly 21+ recommended pairing.

name: Rhubarb Pistachio
desc: Tart rhubarb from a family farm in the Willamette Valley, roasted pistachios, and a hint of cardamom. Pink, green, and absolutely spring.

name: Blackberry Sage
desc: Wild-picked Oregon blackberries with fried sage leaves in brown butter. Savory-sweet and tasted like the Pacific Northwest in a cookie.

name: Hazelnut Praline
desc: Oregon hazelnuts (99% of US crop) candied in-house with caramel and sea salt. Crunchy, buttery, and unmistakably local. A love letter to the Willamette Valley.
